title: Applying the Stats NZ revisions policy to outputs from the Household Economic Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## Applying the Stats NZ revisions policy to outputs from the Household Economic Survey
03 February 2022, 3:00pm
29 May 2026 : Stats NZ has replaced the revisions policy with its data updates policy. Applying the Stats NZ data updates policy to outputs from the Household Economic Survey explains how the data updates policy is applied to HES outputs.
This paper explains how the Stats NZ revisions policy is applied to the Household Economic Survey (HES). This applies to all published outputs from HES, including child poverty statistics.
Policy for revising previously published statistics guides how Stats NZ manages revisions to data, statistics, insights and other information it releases.

## Why Stats NZ revises outputs from the Household Economic Survey
The application and publication of revisions to outputs from the Household Economic Survey (HES) helps ensure the accuracy and integrity of the dataset and is in keeping with statistical best practice.
To maintain the trust of users of the data, revisions – and explanations for why they are done – need to be communicated transparently. The larger the revisions, or the longer the time frame to which they may apply, the greater the need to inform users of the pending revisions before their publication.
Stats NZ’s revisions policy recognises:
- revisions due to changes in input data (including updated administrative data, population estimates, and household living-costs price indexes)
- revisions due to major conceptual or methodological changes.
While this policy applies to all outputs derived from HES, its specific application depends on the nature of the revision.
The social and economic welfare of New Zealanders is measured through income, expenditure, housing costs, net worth, and non-monetary measures. HES Income is the base component of each collection period. In three-year cycles, extensive additional questions related to expenditure and to net worth are separately included alongside income.
Specifically, HES provides annual information on:
- the distribution of household gross and disposable income
- the economic resources available to households after accounting for housing costs
- the distribution of non-monetary material wellbeing and hardship
Every three years, HES also provides information on:
- average weekly household expenditure
- itemised household expenditure (which supports weighting for the consumers price index)
- the distribution and composition of net worth (assets minus liabilities) in New Zealand.

## Revisions policy guidance
The application of the revisions policy to HES is documented below. In considering revisions, we try to balance the user’s need for consistency with diminishing returns on accuracy. Any decision regarding a revision will consider its impact on published outputs and on users’ expectations or actions.
If we have revised estimates from a previous cycle of HES, the updated estimates will be published alongside the estimates for the current year and will be denoted with an R in the relevant tables and charts.
The Child Poverty Reduction Act 2018 stipulates that the original estimates should be used to monitor progress, with 2017/18 estimates used as fixed baseline figures.

### Revisions due to changes in input data
The most recent year of HES will always include the most up-to-date data available.
The decision on whether to revise, and re-release, previous estimates will take the child poverty reporting and monitoring framework into account and will be based on the magnitude of the change, along with maintaining the public’s trust and confidence in the integrity of published statistics. The importance of preserving the time series is another crucial factor in any such decision.
Updates to any or all of the following three data sources will result in a revision of previously published HES outputs.
- Population estimates
- Household living-costs price indexes.
IDI data is updated on a regular basis as more recent and more accurate data becomes available for the time period through the tax system. Previous estimates will be re-run using this new data every year, and at minimum the HES outputs for the most recent year will be revised.
Population estimates change annually because of changes in net migration. The revision to the HES outputs will occur annually after migration numbers have been confirmed. HES will also be revised every five years when updated population benchmarks are available from the census.
Household living-costs price indexes (HLPIs) are used as an inflator to move the fixed line measures and are reviewed every three years alongside the CPI review. Household spending patterns change over time as tastes, lifestyles, and incomes change, and as the range of goods and services available to households changes.

### Revisions due to methodological changes
Major conceptual or methodological changes tend to be irregular, but as they can affect time series analyses, they should be announced in advance where practical.
Such a revision was implemented in HES for the year ended June 2020, when the period for which Working for Families income was counted was re-aligned with other sources of income. More information on this correction can be found in the technical appendix for the year ended June 2020.
Establishing a new administrative data source for HES would be another example of methodological change.
Deciding how far back to revise outputs depends on several factors. At minimum, the current and previous year of HES will be revised to validate annual changes. Some revisions will go back to the start of the series (2018/19) while others may go to the baseline year (2017/18), or even the full back series (2007).
The type of change required will determine when a revision is implemented.
Frequency of revision
Frequency of revision
Updated administrative data Updated population estimates (benchmarks)
- Updated administrative data
- Updated population estimates (benchmarks)
Updated population estimates (census), revised back to HES survey at time of last census
- Updated population estimates (census), revised back to HES survey at time of last census
Latest household living-costs price indexes for the fixed line measures Identified methodological improvements, fore example new administrative data sources to be included in the data
- Latest household living-costs price indexes for the fixed line measures
- Identified methodological improvements, fore example new administrative data sources to be included in the data

## Net worth and expenditure revisions
The three types of revision indicated above also apply to the measures of net worth and expenditure. Due to the three-yearly rotational basis of both outputs, the revision timeline is restricted (see below).
Frequency of revision
Frequency of revision
Every release (three years)
Every release (three years)
Updated population estimates (benchmarks or census). In the case of census rebases, we will revise back to at least the last census for which estimates are available. Identified methodological improvements
- Updated population estimates (benchmarks or census). In the case of census rebases, we will revise back to at least the last census for which estimates are available.
- Identified methodological improvements
Identified methodological improvements, for example new administrative data sources to be included in the data
- Identified methodological improvements, for example new administrative data sources to be included in the data
